---
title: "Data Dictionary for Facility Reporting Variability Indicators"
format:
  pdf:
    pdf-engine: lualatex
    mainfont: "Work Sans"
    fontsize: 12pt
    geometry: margin=1in
    number-sections: false
    colorlinks: true
    linkcolor: blue
    toc: false
---

## `facility`
Name of the Iowa trauma facility. Used as the primary grouping variable for reporting and anomaly detection.

## `2024`
Total number of trauma registry records submitted for calendar year 2024.

## `2025`
Total number of trauma registry records submitted for calendar year 2025.

## `diff_2025`
Year-to-year differences in reporting, calculated as $current_{year}-prior_{year}$.

## `mean_records`
Mean annual reporting volume across 2020–2025.

## `mean_diff`
Mean of annual year-to-year differences across 2021–2025.

## `total_2020_2025`
Total number of trauma registry records submitted from 2020–2025.

## `pct_2025`
Proportional year-to-year change, calculated as $\frac{(current_{year} − previous_{year})}{previous_{year}}$.

## `z_score_diff_2025`
Standardized year-to-year difference relative to each facility’s historical variability.

## `z_anomaly_2025`
Indicator for whether the standardized year-to-year change meets the anomaly threshold $(|Z| ≥ 1.5)$.

## `pct_anomaly_2025`
Indicator for whether the proportional year-to-year change exceeds $±50\%$.

## `date_data`
Calendar date (YYYY‑MM‑DD) on which the dataset was refreshed. Documents the data extraction date used for the reporting period.
